extension.description: Test Explorer, CodeLens, and schema-aware authoring for Tarn API test files.
capabilities.untrustedWorkspaces.descrip: In untrusted workspaces, Tarn provides read-only features (grammar, snippets, schema validation). Running tests, validating files, and spawning the Tarn binary are disabled.

walkthrough.gettingStarted.title: Get started with Tarn
walkthrough.gettingStarted.description: Run your first Tarn test, inspect failures, and customize your workspace.
walkthrough.step.install.title: Install the Tarn CLI
walkthrough.step.install.description:
The extension drives the `tarn` binary. Install it from the [project README](https://github.com/NazarKalytiuk/tarn#install) and verify with `tarn --version`.
[Open install instructions](command:tarn.installTarn)
walkthrough.step.openExample.title: Open or create a test file
walkthrough.step.openExample.description:
Tarn test files use the `.tarn.yaml` extension. Open an existing file or create `tests/health.tarn.yaml` to get started.
[Create new file](command:workbench.action.files.newUntitledFile)
walkthrough.step.runFirst.title: Run your first test
walkthrough.step.runFirst.description:
Open the Test Explorer to see discovered tests and click Run, or run the active file directly.
[Run current file](command:tarn.runFile)
walkthrough.step.pickEnv.title: Select an environment
walkthrough.step.pickEnv.description:
Add a `tarn.env.staging.yaml` next to your tests and pick it from the status bar to run against that environment.
[Select environment](command:tarn.selectEnvironment)
walkthrough.step.inspectFailure.title: Inspect a failure
walkthrough.step.inspectFailure.descript: When a step fails, hover the red marker or open the Test Explorer entry to see expected vs actual, full request, full response, and remediation hints.
configuration.title: Tarn
configuration.tarn.binaryPath.descriptio: Path to the tarn CLI binary. Use an absolute path if it is not on PATH.
configuration.tarn.backend.description: Which backend to use for running tests. `cli` spawns `tarn` per command; `mcp` keeps a `tarn-mcp` process alive and speaks JSON-RPC.
configuration.tarn.backend.enum.cli: Spawn the `tarn` CLI for every command (default)
configuration.tarn.backend.enum.mcp: Keep a `tarn-mcp` process alive and dispatch commands as JSON-RPC requests
configuration.tarn.mcpPath.description: Override path to `tarn-mcp` binary (otherwise resolved from PATH or bundled binary).
configuration.tarn.lspBinaryPath.descrip: Path to the tarn-lsp binary used by the experimental vscode-languageclient integration. Use an absolute path if it is not on PATH.
configuration.tarn.experimentalLspClient: Experimental: start tarn-lsp as a side-by-side LSP client. Direct providers remain active until Phase V2 migrates each feature individually. Requires the tarn-lsp binary to be installed. Default: false.
configuration.tarn.testFileGlob.descript: Glob pattern used to discover Tarn test files.
configuration.tarn.defaultEnvironment.de: Default Tarn environment passed as --env. Overridden by the active environment selection.
configuration.tarn.defaultTags.descripti: Default tag filter applied to runs.
configuration.tarn.requestTimeoutMs.desc: Process-level watchdog (ms) for tarn invocations. Marked machine-overridable so a slow Remote SSH / WSL / Dev Container host can set a longer timeout without leaking that value back into the local workspace.
configuration.tarn.showCodeLens.descript: Show Run and Dry Run CodeLens actions above each test and step.
configuration.tarn.statusBar.enabled.des: Show the Tarn status bar entry.
configuration.tarn.validateOnSave.descri: Run `tarn validate --format json` on every save of a .tarn.yaml and surface parse errors as diagnostics in the Problems panel.
configuration.tarn.notifications.failure: When to show a warning notification after a failing Tarn run. Dry runs never trigger this notification.
Always show a warning toast after a failing run
Only show when the Tarn activity bar is not focused (default)
configuration.tarn.cookieJarMode.descrip: Cookie jar isolation mode. `per-test` forces the CLI `--cookie-jar-per-test` flag on every run so subset runs (run-at-cursor, run-step) never inherit stale session state from tests that happen not to be in scope.
configuration.tarn.cookieJarMode.enum.de: Honor the file's declared `cookies:` mode (shared jar by default)
configuration.tarn.cookieJarMode.enum.pe: Pass --cookie-jar-per-test to every tarn run so the default jar is reset between named tests
